titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/217

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=485 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:53:00.107609+00 | translation moa=486 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:52:57.409275+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte Matris ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Henricus de beletun salutem . Nouerit uniuersitas uestra me dedisse concessis
se et hac carta mea confirmasse monachis de Melros totam terram meam de kingissete in territorio de panneselis sicut iacet
inter calueburne et kingeburn’ et witeddre tenendam et habendam de me et heredibus meis ad feudam firmam in perpetuum . Reddendo
inde michi et heredibus meis singulis annis duas marcas argenti . vnam scilicet ad festum sancti Michaelis . et alteram ad pentecosten .
Ego quidem et heredes mei manutenebimus et warantizabimus et defendemus predictis Monachis totam predictam terram cum omnibus
libertatibus et pertinenciis suis et adquietabimus erga omnes homines ab omni seruicio . consuetudine . exactione et demanda . Ita quod predicti
Monachi predictam terram libere et quiete tenebunt et habebunt in perpetuum per predictum redditum duarum Marcarum . Actum anno gracie
millesimo . ducentesimo . tricesimo primo . Hiis Testibus . Domino Iohanne de Maccuswell’ Camerario Regis . Domino Thoma filio Ranulfi . Domino . Bern’ de
haudene . Domino Iohanne de Metkil . Philippo de pethcox . Et Multis aliis .

To all sons of Holy Mother Church, present and to come, Henry of Belton sends greeting. Let your whole community know me to have given, granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, to the monks of Melrose my whole land of Kingside in the territory of Penshiel, as it lies between Kell Burn and King Burn and Whiteadder; to be held and had of me and my heirs at feu ferme for ever; by paying from it to me and my heirs each year two merks of silver, that is one at the feast of St Michael, and the other on Whitsunday. I and my heirs shall assuredly maintain and warrant and defend to the said monks the whole aforesaid land with all its liberties and pertinents and we shall discharge claims towards all men from every service, custom, exaction and demand; so that the said monks shall hold and have the said land freely and quitely for ever by the siad rent of two merks. Enacted in the year of grace 1231. With these witnesses: the lord Joh of Maxwell, king's chamberlain; the lord Thomas son of Ranulf; the lord Bernard of Howden; the lord John of Methkil; Philip of Pitcox; and many others.
